Today's NBA Odds - Bet on NBA Spreads, Moneylines and Live Odds

At FanDuel Sportsbook, we provide a user-friendly platform for basketball fans eager to bet on NBA odds throughout the season. Our online sportsbook offers a wide range of betting options, including NBA game lines, NBA championship odds, various NBA prop bets, and futures. To cater to beginners and experienced bettors alike, we present straightforward NBA betting lines, moneylines, and over/under odds for a simple betting experience. FanDuel Sportsbook also lets you try your hand at NBA parlays and Same Game Parlays, which can lead to larger payouts by combining multiple bets.

Stay ahead of the game by keeping an eye on this odds page for the latest NBA odds and betting opportunities. Dive into the exciting world of NBA betting, explore prop bets for stars like LeBron James, Giannis Antetokounmpo, Stephen Curry, and Jayson Tatum, and find the best odds for your favorite NBA teams all season long. Experience the fun of building parlays, Same Game Parlays, and discovering thousands of unique NBA player prop bets, all at FanDuel Sportsbook.


Popular Ways to Bet on NBA

When betting on NBA, you'll come across several popular bet types that cater to different preferences and strategies. Here's a quick rundown of the most common ways to bet on the National Basketball League at FanDuel Sportsbook:


  • NBA Spreads: Betting on the point spreads of NBA games. These NBA odds require a team to win or lose by a specific number of points, evening the playing field between unevenly matched teams.

  • NBA Moneylines: Betting on the win/loss outcome of the game, with no regard to the point difference.

  • NBA Totals: Also known as over/under bets. This involves predicting if the total points scored by both teams will be over or under the projected point total.

  • Live NBA Odds: Bet on NBA games while they're being played, with updated moneylines and point spread odds as the game progresses.

  • NBA Player Props: These bets focus on the performance of individual players, rather than the final outcome. Examples include betting on a player's number of points, assists, three-pointers, or rebounds in a game. You can find prop bets in the "More Wagers" section of our odds tables.

  • NBA Championship Odds: NBA futures bet on which team will win the NBA Championship and which teams will play in the NBA Finals. You can also find other NBA futures odds such as NBA conference and division winner odds.

  • NBA Player Awards Odds: Betting on who will win the MVP, Rookie of the Year, Defensive Player of the Year, Sixth Man of the Year, Most Improved Player, and Coach of the Year.

  • NBA Parlays: Combining multiple bets into one for a chance to increase your payout. You can choose to parlay NBA odds from different games or do an NBA Same Game Parlay, combining various bets within the same game.


Bet on NBA Odds All Year at FanDuel Sportsbook

FanDuel Sportsbook offers NBA odds all year round, with something always available for basketball enthusiasts and casual fans. Here's a quick rundown of what we offer in our online sportsbook:

During the NBA season (October to April), you can bet on moneyline, spread, over/under odds, player and game props, and even create parlays with all of these. Key betting periods include Christmas Day, trade deadline, All-Star Weekend, and the end-of-the-season playoff race. NBA futures, such as Championship odds, Award odds, and Conference and Division Winners, are also available throughout the season.

When the NBA Playoffs begin in April, the excitement intensifies as the stakes get higher for every team in the playoffs. Keep an eye on daily line movements in NBA Championship odds, enjoy fun prop bets related to each playoff series, or bet on the same game betting lines that we offer for every NBA matchup.

Post-season, bet on NBA Draft odds and NBA futures like Championship odds and Win Totals. Look for value by anticipating team improvements through Free Agency and the NBA Draft. Summer League odds and early regular-season game bets will also be available as Opening Night nears.


Live Betting and Live Odds

FanDuel Sportsbook offers live sports betting on NBA odds. Live betting means we will provide you with up-to-the-second betting lines on every basketball game this season, which will allow you to place live bets on NBA spreads, moneylines, and over/under totals as the games are occurring. Keep a look out between stoppages and during commercial breaks, as the live odds can change quickly during a basketball game. A crucial turnover or quick three-pointer can quickly change your strategy in NBA betting. Follow the game closely and see if you can predict the outcomes correctly!


How to Read NBA Odds


How NBA Point Spreads Work

Similar to our online sports betting odds for other sports, you will see either a plus sign (+) or a minus sign (-) next to the NBA spreads to indicate the likelihood of that team winning. When you read NBA spreads, the favored team will have a point spread with a minus sign and the underdog team will have a point spread with a plus sign.

For example, the favorite team to win the game could have a spread of -4.5. This means they must win by five or more points to cover the spread. The underdog for the game would be set at +4.5 which means they must lose by less than five points or win the game outright.

How NBA Moneylines Work

When making a NBA moneyline bet, or a straight bet, you are betting which team will win outright. Because there isn’t a point spread, the payout will change based on the moneyline odds. For the favorite, the odds to win is the dollar amount you must bet to get a $100 payout. For the underdog, you must bet $100 to get the odds number as a payout. Here’s how it would look if the Boston Celtics are a -145 favorite and the Los Angeles Lakers are a +130 underdog:

  • A $145 bet on the Boston Celtics would win $100 (not including the $145 you wagered)
  • A $100 bet on the Los Angeles Lakers would win $135 (not including the $100 you wagered)

Wagering on the sports betting favorite forces you to bet more money than the potential profit you'll receive. This is called “laying money”. If you are laying money consistently, then you’ll need to win these NBA bets over 50% of the time to turn a profit.


Basketball Betting FAQs


  • How are NBA betting results determined?

    NBA betting results are based on the statistics provided by the league's governing body (www.nba.com) and the official data supplier.

  • What happens to player props market bets if a player doesn't play?

    If a player doesn't take the court, all bets on that player will be void. Bets stand once a player takes the court, regardless of game time played, and include overtime.

  • Does overtime count for all NBA betting markets?

    Yes, overtime counts for all NBA betting markets unless stated otherwise.

  • How are game props settled?

    Game props are settled based on the player with the most of the relevant statistic. Dead heat rules apply in case of a tie. If a listed player doesn't participate, bets on that player are void. Bets are All-In; if an unlisted player has the highest statistic, all bets are settled as a loss.

  • Where can I find all the rules for NBA betting?

    You can find all the rules for NBA betting in the House Rules section, located in the sportsbook menu.

The Boston Celtics have surged into the second spot at +450, followed by the San Antonio Spurs at +550 odds. The Cleveland Cavaliers round out the top contenders with +1400 odds.


At FanDuel Sportsbook, we're bringing the excitement of the Western Conference Playoffs and the Eastern Conference Playoffs as the best teams in the league battle it out for a chance at the championship. This year's postseason is delivering high-stakes matchups across both conferences, and you can stay close to the action with a wide variety of NBA Playoffs odds. From picking series winners to building a Same Game Parlay on tonight's marquee matchup, FanDuel offers thousands of ways to engage with the best players in the world.

How NBA Finals Spreads and Moneylines Work

Just like during the regular season, you can bet on every NBA Finals game with spreads, totals, and moneylines.

  • A favorite like OKC might be listed at -4.5, meaning they must win by 5+ points to cover. Likewise, Indiana being a +4.5 point underdog means they can "cover" the spread by losing by 4 points or less, or winning the game outright.
  • Moneyline odds of -200 indicate that you need to bet $200 to win $100. A +350 underdog line means a $100 bet would return $350 profit.

FanDuel Sportsbook ensures full transparency with live odds, bet slip calculators, and responsible gambling resources available at all times.

Why Bet NBA Finals with FanDuel Sportsbook?


  • Trusted Market Leader: Official betting partner of the NBA.
  • Real-Time Odds: Adjusted by in-house analysts and informed by live market activity.
  • Flexible Markets: From player props to live Same Game Parlays and Finals MVP futures.
  • Responsible Gaming Tools: Deposit limits, timeouts, and support always available.

NBA Finals Betting FAQs


  • Does overtime count toward my NBA Finals bets?

    Yes. For nearly every Finals market (moneyline, spreads, totals, margin, “last point,” etc.) all statistics include any overtime periods unless the market header clearly says otherwise. The main exception is a handful of specialty markets (e.g., Team Threes Made) that specify “overtime not included.”

  • Will my player prop be void if the listed player doesn’t take the court?

    For NBA Finals player props, the player must step on the court in at least one game of the series for the bet to stand. If they never play, all wagers involving that player are void. In multi-player “match-bet” props or parlay specials, each listed player must meet the same participation requirement (and some Parlay Specials also need the player to appear in at least 50% of games).

  • What happens if two players tie in a NBA Finals prop such as “Most Points in the Series”?

    When a prop ends in a tie, FanDuel applies dead-heat rules. Your stake is divided by the number of tied winners and then settled at the original odds, so you still get paid—just on a proportionally reduced stake.

  • Which statistics does FanDuel use to grade NBA Finals wagers, and do later stat corrections change my payout?

    All bets are settled using the results and box-score data published by the NBA’s official governing body and its data supplier. Once FanDuel grades a market, subsequent league stat corrections do not alter the outcome of your wager.

NBA Team Props: Regular Season Win Totals Over/Under

NBA Win Totals are a type of NBA Futures market in which you are betting on the total number of wins an NBA team will have for the 2025-2026 basketball season. You will decide if the team will have more or less wins than what the FanDuel Sportsbook predicts. These bets can be made throughout the season, but the odds will adjust throughout the season as injuries, transactions, and other news breaks. Once your bet is placed, your wager will be settled at the end of the season.

How To Read NBA Regular Season Win Totals

In the odds listed above, you’ll see two separate numbers for each team. You’ll see a number, such as 44.5, which indicates the number of wins a team is predicted to have. With this, you can either pick to bet if they will go over or under that number of wins during the regular season. Preseason games, Playoff games, and Play-In games do not count towards the Win Total.

The second number you see will be a three digit number with a plus (+) sign or a minus (-) sign. With a plus (+) sign, a $100 bet will payout the number following the sign. For a minus (-) sign, you have to bet the number to win $100. To make your payout calculation easier, try adding picks to your betslip and we’ll do the math for you!

What Is An NBA Parlay Bet

A parlay is a bet that combines two or more selections into one larger bet. For a parlay to be successful, each of the individual legs (or selections) must win. With each added leg, your payout will increase, allowing you to get large payouts with lower wager amounts.

Sportsbook Odds / NBA Odds / NBA Player Props

NBA Player Props: Points, 3s, Assists, and Rebounds

Explore the latest NBA player prop odds and find your edge with FanDuel Sportsbook. Whether you're tracking elite scorers, sharpshooters from deep, or playmakers on the glass, our NBA player prop markets offer a dynamic way to bet on individual performances on every night of the season.

What Are NBA Player Props?

NBA player props let you bet on how specific players will perform in a game across key stat categories:

  • Points – Total points scored by a player.
  • 3-Pointers Made – Over/Under on made threes.
  • Assists – Total dimes dished out.
  • Rebounds – Combined offensive and defensive boards.

Unlike traditional team bets, props give you a chance to focus on individual matchups, trends, and game scripts. Whether you're backing Steph Curry to make over 4.5 threes, or banking on Nikola Jokic to hit a double-digit assist total, FanDuel has the odds ready and updated in real time.

Why NBA Player Props Are Popular


  • Matchup-Based Strategy: Player props reward detailed analysis. Experts look at who’s guarding who, game pace, injury news, etc.
  • Live-Betting Opportunities: Adjust your positions during the game with our live player prop odds.
  • Same Game Parlays: Combine multiple props into one bet with FanDuel’s SGP builder—like LeBron James Over 25.5 points + Over 6.5 assists.

Why Bet NBA Player Props at FanDuel Sportsbook?


  • Trusted Expertise: Backed by professional oddsmakers and official data partnerships with the NBA.
  • Real-Time Updates: Live odds and betting splits help you make informed decisions.
  • Bet Tools and Insights: View trends, player stat logs, and prop history right on our sportsbook before you lock in.
  • Responsible Gaming Tools: Deposit limits, timeouts, and support always available.

NBA Player Props FAQs


  • Do overtime periods count toward my player-prop totals (points, rebounds, assists, etc.)?

    Yes. Unless the market header explicitly says “overtime not included,” all standard NBA player props are settled on the full box-score statistics including every overtime period played.

  • What happens to my wager if the listed player never steps on the court?

    For single-game player props, the athlete must appear in the game for the bet to stand. If they do not play at all, FanDuel voids that selection and returns your stake (or recalculates any parlay).

  • How does FanDuel settle a player prop when two or more athletes tie on the same statistic?

    Ties trigger dead-heat rules: your stake is divided by the number of tied winners and then paid out at the original odds. You still get paid—just on a proportionally reduced stake.

  • Which data source does FanDuel use to grade NBA player props, and do later stat corrections change my payout?

    All NBA player props are graded using the official statistics published by the league and its data supplier. Once FanDuel settles a market, subsequent NBA stat corrections do not alter the result of your bet.

  • How is a “First Basket” player prop decided?

    The “First Basket” market is settled on the first made field goal or free throw of the game (own goals and technical-foul shots do not count). If the basket is waved off by an official review, the next valid score determines the winner.

  • What happens to a Same Game Parlay if my NBA Player Prop leg is voided?

    If a player-specific leg is void (e.g., the player didn’t play), FanDuel recalculates the parlay at the combined odds of the remaining legs; the bet remains live unless all legs are void, in which case your full stake is refunded.

NBA Futures Same Game Parlay Betting: 2026-27 Season Odds

Dive into the excitement of the upcoming 2026-27 NBA season with FanDuel Sportsbook's NBA Futures Same Game Parlay (SGP) betting options. Here, you can place strategic wagers on NBA Championship winner, Conference and Division winners, Regular Season Win Totals, teams to make the Play-In Tournament, and even the 2026 NBA Cup winner. Futures bets offer a unique opportunity to engage with the entire NBA season, providing potentially significant payouts as odds fluctuate with team performance, trades, and injuries.

FanDuel Sportsbook allows you to combine multiple predictions from the future NBA season into a single Same Game Parlay, amplifying the excitment and potential rewards. Whether you're tracking the top championship contenders or eyeing a dark horse team, FanDuel provides dynamic odds and a seamless betting experience.


Understanding NBA Futures Same Game Parlay Odds

NBA Futures bets are wagers placed on events that will occur later in the season, often before the season even begins. Unlike daily game betting, futures require a long-term perspective. A Same Game Parlay (SGP) takes this a step further, allowing you to combine multiple selections from these various future markets into one single bet. For example, you might parlay a wager on the Boston Celtics to win the Eastern Conference with their Regular Season Win Total going over 50.5, and the New York Knicks to win the Atlantic Division.

Odds are presented in American moneyline format:

  • A +200 line means a $100 bet would return $200 in profit.
  • A -150 line means you would need to bet $150 to win $100.
  • The higher the number after the plus sign, the less likely the outcome is perceived, and the larger the potential payout.

Keep in mind that combining multiple legs into an SGP increases the potential payout but also the risk, as every selection must hit for the parlay to win.

NBA All-Star Game Odds & Betting Lines

Get ready for the league's annual mid-season celebration with the latest NBA All-Star Game odds and betting lines at FanDuel Sportsbook. Each year, the NBA All-Star Game brings together 48 of the league's brightest stars for an exhibition of unparalleled talent and excitement. Beyond the main event, the weekend features the Rising Stars Challenge, showcasing the NBA's most promising young talent, along with various skills competitions.

FanDuel Sportsbook is your premier destination for betting on every facet of the NBA All-Star festivities. Whether you're looking to wager on the 2026 NBA All-Star Winning Team, the Rising Stars Challenge victor, or individual game outcomes, you'll find competitive odds and a wide array of betting markets right here.


Who Has the Best Odds to Win the 2026 NBA All-Star Game?

As the 2026 NBA All-Star Game approaches on February 15, 2026, bettors are already eyeing the favorites. FanDuel Sportsbook offers dynamic futures odds for the overall winning team, with options such as "World," "USA Stripes," and "USA Stars." These odds will shift as the event draws closer, reflecting player form, team composition, and betting volume.

Similarly, for the Rising Stars Challenge on February 13, 2026, you can bet on outright winners like "Team Melo," "Team T-Mac," "Team Vince," and "Team Austin." Stay tuned to FanDuel Sportsbook for real-time updates on which teams are leading the pack.


How NBA All-Star Game Betting Works

FanDuel Sportsbook offers a comprehensive range of betting options for the NBA All-Star Game and its accompanying events:

  • Outright Winner: Bet on which team will win the main NBA All-Star Game (e.g., "World") or the Rising Stars Challenge.
  • Individual Game Spreads: Wager on the point differential in specific All-Star games. For example, if "USA Stars" are +2.5 (-113), they can lose by 2 points or fewer, or win outright, for your bet to cash.
  • Individual Game Moneylines: Simply pick which team you believe will win an individual All-Star game outright. A "World" moneyline of -196 means you'd bet $196 to win $100 profit.
  • Individual Game Totals (Over/Under): Predict whether the combined score of both teams in an All-Star game will go over or under a specified total (e.g., O 84.5 or U 82.5).
  • Player Props: Dive into individual player performances, betting on over/under for points, assists, or rebounds in the All-Star Game or Rising Stars Challenge.

NBA All-Star Betting FAQs


  • What is the format of the NBA All-Star Game?

    The format has evolved, historically featuring East vs. West conferences. More recently, it has adopted a captain-drafted team format and often includes a target score for the final quarter, known as the Elam Ending, to add competitive intensity.

  • How do the exhibition nature of All-Star games affect betting?

    All-Star games are primarily for entertainment, meaning defensive intensity is typically lower than regular-season or playoff games. This often leads to higher-scoring contests, which can be a key factor when considering spread and total bets. Player motivation can also vary, with some playing to impress and others conserving energy.

  • What types of bets are available for the Rising Stars Challenge?

    For the Rising Stars Challenge, FanDuel offers outright winner bets (e.g., "Team Melo"), as well as individual game spreads, moneylines, totals (Over/Under), and even player prop bets on rookie and sophomore performances.

  • How are players selected for the NBA All-Star Game?

    Starters are determined by a combination of fan, player, and media voting. The NBA head coaches then select the reserves for each conference, ensuring a balanced representation of the league's top talent.

  • Can I bet on other events during All-Star Weekend?

    Beyond the main All-Star Game and Rising Stars Challenge, FanDuel often offers odds for other popular All-Star Weekend events, including the NBA All-Star Game MVP, the Slam Dunk Contest, the Three-Point Contest, and the Skills Challenge.

FanDuel offers a comprehensive array of betting markets, allowing you to wager on outright winners, exciting player props, and much more, all with real-time odds curated by our expert analysts.


Who Has the Best Odds to Win NBA All-Star Weekend Events?

The NBA All-Star Weekend presents a unique betting landscape, with odds fluctuating based on participant announcements, recent player performance, and betting volume. For the dynamic Rising Stars Challenge, keep an eye on the outright winner odds for teams comprised of the league's top rookies and sophomores. In the electrifying Three-Point Contest, sharpshooters like Kon Knueppel, Damian Lillard, and Devin Booker are often among the favorites, with their odds reflecting their proven accuracy.

As the event approaches, FanDuel Sportsbook continuously updates its NBA betting odds for all competitions, including the Slam Dunk Contest and Skills Challenge. Whether you're backing a well-known star or identifying a dark horse, our platform ensures you have access to the most current lines for every facet of the NBA All-Star Weekend.


Understanding NBA All-Star Weekend Betting Markets

Betting on the NBA All-Star Weekend offers a diverse range of market types for every kind of bettor:

  • Outright Winner Bets: The most straightforward wager, where you select which player or team will win a specific event, such as the Rising Stars Challenge or the Three-Point Contest.
  • Player Prop Bets: Dive deeper into individual performances. These can include "To Qualify for the Final" odds in the Three-Point Contest, specific round scores, or even Over/Under lines on individual player statistics for the All-Star Game itself (e.g., points, assists, rebounds).
  • Parlay Bets: Combine multiple selections from different events or markets into a single wager for a higher potential payout. For example, you might parlay a player to win the Three-Point Contest with another player to qualify for the final. All legs of a parlay must hit for the bet to win.
  • Futures Bets: Look ahead with bets on long-term outcomes, such as who will be crowned the NBA All-Star Game MVP, or even early odds for participants in next season's events. These markets often provide excellent value for keen observers.

Understanding Jayson Tatum's First Basket Odds and Bet Types

FanDuel Sportsbook provides competitive odds on various methods for Jayson Tatum to score the first basket in a given game during the 2025-26 NBA season. Each option comes with specific odds reflecting its implied probability and potential payout:

  • Three Point Field Goal (+190): Tatum kicks off the scoring with a shot from beyond the arc.
  • Other (+190): Any scoring method not explicitly listed, such as a mid-range jumper or a tip-in.
  • Free Throw (+470): Tatum draws a foul early and scores from the charity stripe.
  • Layup (+380): A close-range shot, often off a drive to the basket.
  • Dunk (+1600): Tatum electrifies the crowd with a slam to open the game.

These NBA odds are meticulously set by FanDuel's expert oddsmakers, taking into account player statistics, team strategies, and opponent defenses. Analyzing these factors can help you make informed decisions when placing your Jayson Tatum prop bets.

PLAYER_SPECIALS: Resulted only on 2025-26 NBA Regular Season games. Statistics recorded in the NBA In-Season Tournament Championship game are not included
NBA_CUP_GROUP_WINNER: Determined by the 1st Seed of the given NBA Cup Group. Wildcard entrants to the Quarter Finals are excluded.
FIRST_QUARTER_WINNER_/_MATCH_WINNER: Resulted on score at the end of the first quarter and full time, inclusive of overtime.
PLAYER_TO_SCORE_3+_POINTS_IN_THE_FIRST_3: Combine up to 3 selections in a parlay.
PLAYER_TO_SCORE_5+_POINTS_IN_THE_FIRST_3: Combine up to 2 selections in a parlay.
PLAYER_TO_SCORE_3+_POINTS_IN_EACH_QUARTE: Combine up to 4 selections in a parlay.
PLAYER_TO_SCORE_5+_POINTS_IN_EACH_QUARTE: Combine up to 4 selections in a parlay.
PLAYER_TO_SCORE_8+_POINTS_IN_EACH_QUARTE: Combine up to 2 selections in a parlay.
VICTOR_WEMBANYAMA_FEATURED: All selections pertain to Victor Wembanyama in that given game.
Quadruple Double selections pertain to achieving 10+ Points, Rebounds, Assists, Steals and Blocks
TO_SCORE_5+_POINTS: Tap a player name or icon for stats and more betting options
PLAYER_TO_RECORD_A_THREE_IN_THE_FIRST_3_: Combine up to 3 selections in a parlay.

NBA_TO_MAKE_PLAY_IN: Settled off the 4 teams that finish in the 7-10 Seeds, in the given Conference at the conclusion of the NBA Regular Season.

734.148080153: All-in betting. Other selections available on request.
